svg.text: how the sounds were made · where in the mouth · vowel system · dagesh and begadkefat
— · — PLACES OF ARTICULATION · WHERE EACH SOUND IS MADE — · —
ד ת ל נ ס ז
ד ת ל נ ס ז
four letters produced deep in the throat · cannot take dagesh · attract open a-vowels
like Arabic ح — no English equiv
like Arabic ع — no English equiv
resh (ר) behaves like a guttural in many contexts · uvular trill /r/ produced near pharyngeals
— · — BEGADKEFAT · SIX LETTERS WITH TWO SOUNDS EACH — · —
dagesh lene (dot inside letter) = hard stop · no dagesh = soft fricative · occurs after vowel
Modern Hebrew retained only ב/ו כ/ח פ/ף · Biblical Hebrew had all six
Masoretes (7th–10th c. CE) added vowel points to preserve pronunciation · written below/above consonant
Biblical Hebrew: 5 vowel qualities · each long or short · plus ultra-short (hataf) forms
consonant + vowel · דָבָר da-BAR (word) · stress usually final syllable
consonant + vowel + consonant · מֶלֶך ME-lekh (king) · stress often penultimate
— · — CONNECT TO AUDIO DISC · HEAR THESE SOUNDS — · —
the audio disc for Biblical Hebrew demonstrates all consonants in sequence · gutturals with Arabic comparison
then demonstrates all vowel points on a single root · then the Shema (Deut 6:4) read aloud in full
שְמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יְהוָה אֶחָד
Shːmaː Yisrāʾēl YHWH ʾĕlōhēνû YHWH ʾĕḥāḏ · Hear O Israel, YHWH our God, YHWH is One
